---
Dear Dr. MacDonnell,

I trust this message finds you well. Following our recent departmental meeting, I wanted to extend a special invitation to participate in an upcoming roundtable discussion and workshop series focused on the evolving narrative of peace and understanding in post‐conflict societies. Given your extensive research and thoughtful engagement with the complexities of Northern Ireland's modern history, your contributions to this dialogue would be invaluable.

I understand that both our professional endeavors and personal journeys have taught us the importance of resilience and reflection. Please consider this invitation as an opportunity to not only share your expertise but also connect with peers who appreciate the nuanced interplay between our scholarly work and the personal stories that inform it.

Department of History, Queen's University Belfast

---

2. Professional Invitation: The primary purpose is to invite you to a roundtable and workshop series on peace and reconciliation in post‐conflict societies. The emphasis on your scholarly work and experience underlines the value your participation would add to the event.

3. Implicit Personal Acknowledgement: The phrase "personal journeys" and the reference to learning about "resilience and reflection" subtly acknowledge that professional life can be intertwined with personal experiences. Although the email does not explicitly mention any personal struggles, these phrases indicate that the sender is aware that personal challenges can influence our professional roles. This indirect wording allows for a thoughtful nod to the more personal aspects of one’s life without making them the main focus of the invitation.

4. Invitation for Support: By offering to provide further details and a chance to discuss any questions, the email maintains openness for dialogue. This may also serve as a gentle offer of support if any personal matters come to mind as you consider the event's relevance to your work and life experiences.

In summary, the email is a respectful professional invitation with an undercurrent of personal empathy. It nearly hints at the idea that your personal experiences can enrich your scholarly insights, all while keeping the focus on the academic event and collegial networking.
